assertions.description: The drill file contains 2604 total holes: 1484 vias at 0.3mm, 280 vias at 0.305mm, and 840 component THT holes at 1.016mm. The 840 THT holes match 28 panels × 30 holes per board (J1=6, J2=12, J3=12). The via classification via x2_attributes (Plated,PTH,ViaDrill vs ComponentDrill) is correct.

The set contains 9 gerber files covering F.Cu, B.Cu, F.SilkS, B.SilkS, F.Paste, B.Paste, F.Mask, B.Mask, and the outline (.GKO). This is the standard 9-file set for a 2-layer PCB from KiCad. The B.Paste layer is correctly empty (0 flashes, 0 draws) — back-side THT components have no paste. The SMD ratio of 0.78 (78% SMD pads) is consistent with the mix of SMD passives and THT connectors.

Three unique trace widths found: 0.1524mm (6mil, minimum — used for signal routing), 0.4064mm (16mil — mid-weight), 0.5588mm (22mil — power traces). The 6mil minimum is tight but acceptable for a professional PCB fab. The widths are consistent between the gerber analysis and what the PCB analyzer would report from the .kicad_pcb file.

INCORRECT: completeness.missing_required lists 'Edge.Cuts' and complete=false. The file SparkFun_ESP32_Qwiic_Pro_Mini_panelized.GKO is present in the gerber set (1017 draw strokes, Profile aperture function) but its X2 FileFunction attribute was generated as 'Soldermask,Bot' — the same tag as the .GBS file. The analyzer uses the X2 FileFunction to classify layers, not the file extension, so it fails to recognize .GKO as the board outline and instead misclassifies it as a second soldermask layer. This is an analyzer bug: when X2 FileFunction is absent or mismatched for a .GKO/.GML file, the extension should be used as fallback.
